question_groups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 1–10

Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

Call Record Card
Example Caller’s phone number: 03786 439251
Caller’s name: Robert 1 ………….
Address: 27 2 ………… Moortown
Postcode: DP17 5HJ
Date of birth: 3 ……, 1975
Doctor’s name: Dr Craven
Doctor’s address: Moortown Health Centre

Previous history: Similar back problem in June last year
Details of present problem:
• Started 5 …………. ago
• Got worse this 6 ………….
• No abdominal pain
Possible cause: Lifting up a heavy 7 …………

• Has applied heat
• Has taken 8 …………
Advice given:
• Try using 9 …………….
• Go to doctor tomorrow
• Ask for information about 10 …………….

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 11–16

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Preparing for a trip to an outdoor education center

11 A trip to the outdoor education center will help the children

A create good relationships

B manage without adult supervision

C react positively to new challenges

12 To prepare their children for the trip, parents should

A let them try sleeping in a tent at home

B make sure they get enough sleep before they leave

C tell them that they will be in a tent with their friends

13 What do parents need to put in their children's backpack?

A plastic bags

B snacks and biscuits

C a small amount of money

14 What are children allowed to bring on the trip?

A electronic games

B cell phones

C penknives

15 How are parents selected to attend the trip?

A The first ten parents to sign up will be able to go

B Parents' names will be chosen from a box

C Preference will be given to parents with camping skills

16 Payment for the trip

A must be handed to the child's teacher

B can be left at the school office

C should be made directly to the outdoor education center

items.prompt_html: 11 A trip to the outdoor education center will help the children
12 To prepare their children for the trip, parents should

options.label: A
options.text: let them try sleeping in a tent at home

options.label: B
options.text: make sure they get enough sleep before they leave

options.label: C
options.text: tell them that they will be in a tent with their friends

items.prompt_html: 13 What do parents need to put in their children's backpack?
14 What are children allowed to bring on the trip?
15 How are parents selected to attend the trip?

options.label: A
options.text: The first ten parents to sign up will be able to go

options.label: B
options.text: Parents' names will be chosen from a box

options.label: C
options.text: Preference will be given to parents with camping skills

items.prompt_html: 16 Payment for the trip

options.label: A
options.text: must be handed to the child's teacher

options.label: B
options.text: can be left at the school office

options.label: C
options.text: should be made directly to the outdoor education center

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 21–24

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Stone Point: A New Zealand historical site

21 According to Liz, Stone Point played a key role in local history because

A it was used for defence or attack purposes.

B it was the landing point for the first Maori settlers.

C it was developed as a commercial port.

22 Scott and Liz agree Maori originally chose to inhabit Stone Point because of

A its rich soil.

B the supply of fish.

C available building materials.

23 Scott and Liz agree that using Maori terminology in their presentation

A will be of interest to international students.

B is required as a component of their research.

C would be best avoided.

24 When Scott mentions the engines at Stone Point, Liz advises him to

A focus on the human aspect instead.

B check the accuracy of specific dates.

C explain complex technical language.

items.prompt_html: 21 According to Liz, Stone Point played a key role in local history because

options.label: A
options.text: it was used for defence or attack purposes.

options.label: B
options.text: it was the landing point for the first Maori settlers.

options.label: C
options.text: it was developed as a commercial port.

question_groups.instruction:
What do Liz and Scott agree they must do for each of the following sections for their presentation on Stone Point?
Choose SIX correct answers, A–H, next to questions 25–30.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 25–30

Things to do
A provide further description
B find images
C set up an interview
D check author’s details
E check the exact location
F obtain official permission
G be more objective
H reduce it in length

items.prompt_html: 25 Early Maori inhibition of Stone Point
26 The use of Stone Point as a lookout point
27 The reconstruction of the fort
28 Modernisation of the fort in the 1930s-40s
29 Uses of Stone Point from 1950s to the present day
30 The use of Stone Point as public park

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 31–40

Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

Rural Development

The Berbers
● lived in various countries in North Africa
● were driven from their homes during the 12th century
● began` to lead a nomadic life, moving across 31 ……….. and mountains

Different Berber Lifestyles
● some Berbers, known as 32 …………. nomads, moved twice a year
● some Berbers were completely nomadic
● some Berbers settled by oases, producing fruit, vegetables, and 33 ……………. to cook with
● people and belongings were carried by 34 …………
● settled Berbers lived in houses
● nomadic Berbers used tents
● settled communities organised themselves by holding regular meetings in the 35 ……………. of the village

The Tuaregs
● if goatskins were unavailable, tents were made from 36 …………… or palm-leaf mats
● Tuareg labourers were the descendants of 37 …………….
● adult men always wore a 38 …………. veil
● some Tuaregs moved to Burkina Faso with their 39…………….

Tuaregs and Timbuktu
● Timbuktu was settled by Tuaregs around 1000 years ago
● the city built wealth on the salt and gold trades
● some Tuaregs now work as 40 ………….. for the Mallian Department of Tourism
